extractable_fields.description: solar on the roof or not

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR correctly qualified the call to determine the customer's exact needs, safety risks, and right job to book.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the call to ensure the CSR asked the necessary questions to properly qualify the customer's request (including identifying the problem, timeline, system status, and safety concerns).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Thoroughly and accurately qualified the call, capturing all necessary details.
evaluation_criteria.good: Adequately qualified the call, though missed minor, non-critical details.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Missed important qualification questions, leaving some ambiguity about the job.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed to qualify the call correctly, risking a wasted tech visit or customer experience failure.

metrics.description: Measures if the CSR booked the job under the correct service type in the system.
metrics.evaluation_method: Compare the customer's stated issue on the call against the service type selected by the CSR in the dispatch/booking system.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Booked the call under the exact correct service type.
evaluation_criteria.good: Booked under a generally correct service type but could have been more specific.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Booked under a slightly inaccurate service type that may cause minor confusion for the tech.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Booked under completely the wrong service type, resulting in a misaligned tech visit or lost opportunity.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR applied the correct tags to the call record, specifically ensuring all 4 electrical tags OR all 5 roofing tags are applied and not merely defaulted without checking.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the call log in the CRM/dispatch system to ensure all required tags (4 for electrical OR 5 for roofing) were applied based on the call's context, rather than defaulted without verifying. Also look for required optional tags: 'SAFETY-RISK' (for burning smell, shock, sparks), 'CLUB-MEMBER' (if membership verified), and 'PERMIT-REQUIRED' (for panel, new circuit, or replacement work).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: All relevant tags (4 electrical or 5 roofing) were applied accurately and completely, without blindly defaulting.
evaluation_criteria.good: Most relevant tags were applied with no critical omissions.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Missed one or more important tags, or defaulted tags without checking with the customer.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed to tag the call, applied completely inaccurate tags, or left tags missing entirely.

metrics.source_section: CSR Mission; Section 12: What Good Looks Like

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR successfully finalized the booking and set clear expectations before ending the call, including arrival windows, technician notifications, fee waiver policies, trade-specific details, and verifying contact information.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the closing portion of the call to verify the CSR: 1) Verbally confirmed the appointment date and 3-hour arrival window. 2) Stated the customer will receive a text with the technician's name/photo. 3) Explained the fee waiver policy. 4) Delivered the exact trade-specific script. 5) Confirmed the callback number is correct in ServiceTitan.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Flawlessly executed all finalization steps: confirmed date/3-hour window verbally, explained the tech text and fee waiver policy, delivered the correct trade script, and verified the callback number.
evaluation_criteria.good: Adequately completed the finalization steps but slightly paraphrased the required scripts or missed a minor element.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Missed key expectation settings, such as failing to verbally confirm the 3-hour window, omitting the tech text info, skipping the fee waiver policy, or skipping the trade-specific script.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed entirely to set arrival expectations, explain next steps, or confirm final appointment details before hanging up.

metrics.source_section: CSR Mission; Section 9: Finalizing the Booking; Section 12: What Good Looks Like

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative used the mandatory full greeting when answering the call, including both the company name and their own name.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the beginning of the call to verify the CSR stated: 'Thank you for calling Apex Power and Roofing. This is [Your Name], how can I help you today?' Ensure they did not answer with just 'Hello' or 'Apex Power.'

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Delivered the complete standard greeting flawlessly, including the full company name and their own name.
evaluation_criteria.good: Delivered the full greeting with minor, non-impactful deviations from the exact script.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Omitted their own name or significantly abbreviated the company name.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Answered with just 'Hello', 'Apex Power', or failed completely to use a professional, branded greeting.

metrics.description: Measures adherence to the customer identification process, ensuring existing customer profiles are verified (address, membership) or new customer details are completely captured (name, address, callback number, email, homeowner status).
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the call to verify the CSR used the correct process: For existing customers, confirming the address ('I have you at [address]...') and checking membership/history; for new customers, capturing all required contact details and asking 'Is this your primary residence and are you the homeowner?'.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Flawlessly identified the customer type and captured/verified all required information (address confirmation + membership check for existing; all 5 contact fields + homeowner status for new).
evaluation_criteria.good: Captured most necessary information but missed one minor, non-critical detail (e.g., forgot to ask for an email address or failed to mention membership status verbally).
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Missed multiple required fields or failed to verify the homeowner/primary residence status for a new customer.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed entirely to confirm the service address or capture basic contact information, risking dispatching to the wrong location.

metrics.source_section: Customer Identification; Section 12: What Good Looks Like

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR asked the required probing questions to diagnose the customer's issue and identify any immediate safety or damage concerns.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the call to verify the CSR asked: 'What's happening today?', 'When did this start?', 'Is anything actively sparking, leaking, causing damage, or is there a safety concern?', and 'Is the system completely out or partially working?'

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Asked all required diagnostic questions, including the safety/damage check.
evaluation_criteria.good: Asked most of the diagnostic questions to understand the issue, but missed a minor detail like exactly when it started.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Missed multiple diagnostic questions or failed to explicitly ask about safety concerns, sparking, leaking, or damage.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed to ask the standard problem investigation questions, relying solely on the customer's initial statement.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR asked the required questions to identify the decision-maker, confirm their presence at the appointment, and gauge their readiness to proceed with repairs.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the call to verify the CSR asked: 'Will you be home for the appointment?', 'Is anyone else involved in approving work or repairs?', and 'Are you ready to move forward if the technician finds a solution?'

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Asked all three required decision-maker and readiness questions.
evaluation_criteria.good: Asked questions to confirm the decision-maker will be present, but missed asking about their readiness to move forward.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Missed multiple questions regarding the decision-maker's presence and authority to approve work.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed completely to verify if the caller or a decision-maker will be present for the appointment and authorized to approve work.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR verified property access requirements, including the presence of an adult (18+), secured pets, and trade-specific access requirements.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the call to verify the CSR asked if someone 18 or older will be home, if pets are secured, and the relevant trade-specific questions (e.g., panel/attic access for electrical, or gates/HOA/solar for roofing).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Asked all applicable access questions, including age verification, pets, and trade-specific requirements.
evaluation_criteria.good: Asked the primary access questions (18+ and pets) but missed the trade-specific access questions.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Missed multiple access questions, such as failing to verify someone 18+ will be present or that pets are secured.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed to ask any access verification questions.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR appropriately recognizes and handles electrical and active safety emergencies by immediately booking as priority, notifying dispatch/management, and providing crucial safety instructions.
metrics.evaluation_method: Verify that if a customer mentions electrical safety triggers (burning smell, sparks, shock, smoke, flooding + electrical, fire risk, or no power to whole home), the CSR immediately flags the call as an emergency/priority, stops the standard booking flow, notifies the on-call dispatcher or manager, and advises the customer to turn off the main breaker if safe to do so.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Immediately recognized the safety hazard, booked as emergency/priority, notified the on-call dispatcher, and advised the customer to turn off the main breaker if safe.
evaluation_criteria.good: Flagged the call as an emergency and notified dispatch, but missed giving the advice to turn off the main breaker.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Continued significantly with the standard booking flow before realizing the need to flag the situation, or completely failed to notify dispatch or provide safety advice.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed to recognize the emergency triggers, continued standard flow, and missed dispatcher notification and safety warnings.

metrics.source_section: Access; Section 10: After-Hours and Emergency Protocol

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR strictly adheres to the 'Do Not Book' policies and correctly identifies scenarios that require manager approval before proceeding.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the call to ensure the CSR did not book the job without manager approval if any hard rule applied: customer refuses diagnostic/service fee, no decision-maker authorization, no property access, active safety hazard, out-of-scope work, or a repair-only request for a roof 16+ years old (where an estimate must be offered instead).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Accurately identified the hard rule restriction, stopped the standard booking process, and properly sought manager approval or offered an estimate as required.
evaluation_criteria.good: Identified the restriction but hesitated slightly or needed minor correction when explaining the policy or next steps to the customer.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Proceeded significantly into the booking flow before realizing the scenario violated a 'Do Not Book' hard rule.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Violated a hard rule by booking a restricted scenario (e.g., fee refusal, no access, out of scope) without obtaining manager approval.

metrics.source_section: Section 6: Do Not Book — Hard Rules; Section 12: What Good Looks Like

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR checked the customer's revenue history in ServiceTitan to accurately classify the opportunity before ending the call, defaulting to 'OP' if unclear.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the booking record in ServiceTitan to verify the CSR checked the revenue history and assigned the correct opportunity classification, ensuring it was set to 'OP' if the history was unclear.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Accurately checked revenue history and assigned the correct classification, successfully defaulting to 'OP' when history was unclear.
evaluation_criteria.good: Assigned the correct classification but did not clearly verify revenue history, or hesitated before defaulting to 'OP'.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Assigned an incorrect classification because they failed to default to 'OP' when the revenue history was unclear.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed to check revenue history and completely missed classifying the opportunity.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR accurately recorded the job summary using the customer's actual words, rather than the CSR's interpretation.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the booking notes or job summary section to verify the CSR documented the customer's specific issue accurately, specifically utilizing the customer's own words.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Job summary perfectly captures the customer's specific issue in their exact own words.
evaluation_criteria.good: Job summary is accurate but was paraphrased slightly instead of using the customer's exact words.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Job summary is vague, heavily abbreviated, or misses key details of the customer's specific issue.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Job summary is missing entirely or completely misrepresents the customer's stated issue.

metrics.source_section: Level: L1 | L2 | L3 (Chunk 11); Solar: Yes | No (Chunk 13); Section 12: What Good Looks Like

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR correctly assigned the appropriate operational level (L1, L2, or L3) to the call record.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the call log or booking record to verify the CSR explicitly and correctly classified the job as L1, L2, or L3.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Accurately and explicitly assigned the correct job level (L1, L2, or L3).
evaluation_criteria.good: Assigned a job level, but there is minor ambiguity about whether it was the most optimal tier.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Assigned an incorrect job level based on the call details.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed to assign any job level to the record.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR captures and applies all required roofing-specific booking tags, specifically identifying the 'Roof Age'.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the booking record for roofing jobs to verify that the CSR asked for and populated the 'Roof Age' tag with either the customer's best estimate or 'unknown'.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Properly asked for and tagged the Roof Age with the customer's best estimate or 'unknown'.
evaluation_criteria.good: Asked about the roof age but recorded the information in the general job notes instead of using the dedicated booking tag.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Tagged the roof age but failed to actively ask the customer for their best estimate, resorting to guessing.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Completely failed to capture or tag the Roof Age for a roofing job.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR verified and recorded whether the customer has a solar system (Yes or No).
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the call recording and booking documentation to ensure the CSR captured the customer's solar status as 'Yes' or 'No'.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Explicitly verified and accurately documented the solar status (Yes or No) in the designated field or tags.
evaluation_criteria.good: Accurately documented solar status based on call context without explicitly asking the direct question.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Noted solar status in general notes but failed to use the specific Yes/No designation or tag.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed entirely to ask, capture, or document whether the customer has a solar system.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR appropriately recognizes and handles roofing emergencies, such as active interior water damage or ceiling collapse risk.
metrics.evaluation_method: Verify that when a roofing emergency is identified, the CSR books an inspection or emergency tarp, notifies the on-call dispatcher immediately, and advises the customer to move valuables and place containers under active leaks.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Properly booked the call as an emergency/tarp, notified the dispatcher immediately, and advised the customer to protect valuables and use containers.
evaluation_criteria.good: Booked the emergency and notified the dispatcher, but forgot to provide the damage mitigation advice (valuables/containers).
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Booked the emergency but failed to notify the on-call dispatcher promptly or failed to provide safety/mitigation advice.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed to recognize the roofing emergency, treating it as a standard booking without expedited dispatch or damage mitigation advice.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR accurately quotes the after-hours surcharge based on the specific trade, and correctly waives it for club members.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review after-hours calls to ensure the CSR quotes the correct fee ($149 for electrical, $199 for roofing tarp call-out) and accurately waives the fee entirely if the customer is a verified club member.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Accurately quoted the specific after-hours surcharge for the correct trade, or correctly waived it for a verified club member while communicating the benefit.
evaluation_criteria.good: Quoted the correct fee but hesitated, or waived it for a club member without explicitly mentioning it as a membership benefit.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Quoted the standard diagnostic fee instead of the after-hours surcharge, or quoted the wrong trade's surcharge amount.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed to mention any fees for an after-hours call, or improperly charged the after-hours surcharge to a verified club member.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR accurately utilizes the standard scripts and talking points when presented with common customer scenarios or objections, such as pricing inquiries, home warranties, non-urgent issues, and permits.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the call for relevant triggers and verify the CSR responds with the prescribed points: 1) Price over phone: Explains the need for an in-person diagnostic ($89, waived if work is approved). 2) Home warranty: Explains we do not bill them directly but provide a reimbursement report. 3) Not urgent: Offers to schedule a free estimate. 4) Permits: Confirms the company handles the entire process (pulling, scheduling inspection, final sign-off).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Flawlessly delivered the appropriate standard script for the customer's specific scenario or objection, maintaining company policy without yielding to pressure.
evaluation_criteria.good: Responded appropriately to the scenario but slightly paraphrased the script while maintaining the core message and policies.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Missed key details of the scripted policy (e.g., forgot to mention the $89 fee is waived with approved work, or omitted the home warranty report detail).
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed completely to follow the script, such as providing a blind price over the phone, agreeing to bill a home warranty directly, or giving incorrect permit information.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR immediately acknowledges a customer's club membership and reinforces its value by explicitly stating their membership benefits.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the call to verify that upon identifying an Apex Home Protection Plan member, the CSR immediately acknowledges their membership and states their specific benefits (priority scheduling and waived after-hours fees).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Immediately recognized the club member upon identification and explicitly stated their benefits (priority scheduling, waived fees) using the standard script.
evaluation_criteria.good: Acknowledged the membership promptly but missed explicitly stating one of the specific benefits (e.g., mentioned priority scheduling but forgot the fee waiver).
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Noted the membership but delayed acknowledging it to the customer, or mentioned it only in passing without reinforcing the value/benefits.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Failed entirely to verbally acknowledge the caller's club membership status during the interaction.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR correctly qualifies the call to ensure the right job is booked and revenue opportunities are created.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate the call recording to determine if the CSR gathered the correct information to accurately qualify the customer's needs.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Perfectly qualifies the call, asking all necessary questions to identify the right job.
evaluation_criteria.good: Properly qualifies the call with only minor missed details.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Misses key qualifying information, increasing the risk of a misbooked call.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to qualify the call, resulting in a lost opportunity or wrong job booked.

metrics.description: Measures if the CSR booked the call to the correct service type. Incorrect booking costs a tech visit, customer experience failure, and lost opportunity.
metrics.evaluation_method: Cross-reference the customer's stated issue with the final service type booked in the system, ensuring adherence to the Electrical and Roofing Decisioning Matrices (e.g., selecting Priority service for outages, L1 service for 10+ year old panels, or Estimates for upgrades, generators, and 16+ year old roofs instead of repairs).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Books the call accurately to the exact correct service type.
evaluation_criteria.good: Books the call to a generally correct service type but could be more specific.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Books the call to an incorrect but related service type.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Books the call to a completely wrong service type resulting in a wasted tech visit.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR accurately tagged the call within the system.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the CRM/booking system to verify that the call contains the accurate and required tags based on the conversation and customer history. Specifically, verify the CSR checks revenue history in ServiceTitan to accurately apply the OP (Opportunity: < $1,000 spent in last 12 months or if unclear) or NO (No Opportunity: > $1,000 spent in last 12 months) tags, ensures all required Electrical or Roofing Booking Tags are present for the respective trade, and accurately applies optional tags when applicable (e.g., SAFETY-RISK, CLUB-MEMBER, PERMIT-REQUIRED).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: All appropriate tags are accurately applied to the call.
evaluation_criteria.good: Most required tags are applied accurately.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Missing several relevant tags or applying incorrect tags.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to tag the call entirely or tags are completely inaccurate.

metrics.source_section: Version 1.0 | Customer Service Representatives, Tag Criteria, Section 12: What Good Looks Like

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR successfully set the customer's expectations before hanging up the call.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the closing of the call to ensure the CSR clearly states what the customer should expect next, including arrival windows (e.g., 3-hour window), potential costs and fee waiver policies, process (including specific scripts like mentioning on-site options/pricing and permit handling for electrical estimates, and for roofing, stating the estimator will bring everything for a full inspection and requesting solar provider info if applicable), and informing the customer they will receive a text with the technician's name and photo when they are on their way.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Clearly and thoroughly sets accurate customer expectations before concluding the call, including arrival windows, costs, mentioning the technician profile text notification, and executing all trade-specific expectation scripts (e.g., roofing inspection/solar prep, electrical permit handling).
evaluation_criteria.good: Sets basic expectations but misses minor clarifying details (e.g., forgets to mention the technician photo text notification or a minor trade-specific detail).
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Expectations set are vague or partially incorrect.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Hangs up without setting any expectations for the customer.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative uses the fully mandated greeting, including the company name, their own name, and an offer of assistance.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the beginning of the call to verify the representative uses the full script ('Thank you for calling Apex Power and Roofing. This is [Your Name], how can I help you today?') and does not use an abbreviated greeting.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Delivers the complete standard greeting flawlessly, including company name, their name, and an offer to help.
evaluation_criteria.good: Delivers the full greeting but with minor, acceptable wording variations.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Misses a key component of the greeting, such as forgetting to state their name.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Uses a prohibited short greeting such as 'Hello' or just 'Apex Power'.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative properly identifies the customer, either by accurately verifying an existing profile and checking membership status, or by capturing all required contact information and homeownership status for a new customer.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the call to verify the CSR follows the correct flow: for existing customers, confirming the address using the standard phrasing, confirming the callback number is correct in ServiceTitan, and checking membership/history; for new customers, capturing full name, address, callback number (and verifying it is correct in ServiceTitan), email, and asking if it is their primary residence and if they are the homeowner.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Perfectly executes the identification process, capturing or verifying all required information, including homeownership questions or membership checks.
evaluation_criteria.good: Captures or verifies the majority of the information but misses minor details like email or revenue history check.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Misses important steps such as failing to confirm the service address or forgetting to ask the homeownership question for new customers.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails entirely to identify the customer properly, does not confirm the correct address, or skips capturing mandatory contact details.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR effectively uncovers the details of the customer's issue and assesses for any immediate safety concerns or complete system outages.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the call to verify the CSR asks the required diagnostic questions, including what is happening, when the issue started, whether there are active safety concerns (e.g., sparking, leaking, damage, active flooding), and if the system is completely out or partially working. For trade-specific calls, verify the CSR follows the respective Electrical or Roofing Decisioning Matrix (e.g., confirming panel/roof age, isolated vs. widespread, interior damage, storm timing, or solar presence). Crucially, verify that if the customer mentions a severe hazard, the CSR immediately flags the call as an emergency/escalation and aborts the standard booking flow.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Thoroughly assesses the problem by asking all relevant diagnostic questions, including specific matrix questions (e.g., panel/roof age, storm timing, solar presence). Immediately flags severe hazards (e.g., burning smell, sparks, shock, smoke, active flooding) as emergencies.
evaluation_criteria.good: Asks standard questions to understand the problem but may skip a minor detail like asking exactly when the issue started or confirming specific panel/roof age.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Fails to ask about the operational status of the system or misses checking for safety concerns when the issue description warrants it.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to ask any probing questions about the problem, does not check for safety issues, or completely fails to flag critical hazards (sparks, smoke, shock, flooding) as an emergency.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR asks the necessary questions to confirm that the person attending the appointment has the authority and readiness to approve work.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the call to verify the CSR asks if the customer will be home, if anyone else is involved in approving work or repairs, and if they are ready to move forward if the technician finds a solution.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Asks all required questions confirming presence, decision-making involvement, and readiness to proceed.
evaluation_criteria.good: Confirms presence and authority to approve work, but misses the explicit readiness to move forward question.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Only asks if the customer will be home, failing to ask about approval authority or other decision-makers.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to ask any questions regarding the decision-maker's presence or authority.

metrics.source_section: The Decision-Maker, Section 12: What Good Looks Like

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR confirms necessary site access details prior to booking, including age requirements, pet security, and trade-specific accessibility.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the call to verify the CSR asks if someone 18 or older will be home, if pets are secured, and applicable trade-specific access questions (e.g., panel or attic access for electrical; gates, HOA requirements, or solar panels for roofing).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently asks all required general access questions (18+ and pets) plus all relevant trade-specific access questions.
evaluation_criteria.good: Asks the core access questions but misses a minor trade-specific detail (e.g., forgets to ask about HOA requirements).
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Misses critical general access questions, such as confirming if an adult 18 or older will be present.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to ask any access-related questions before attempting to book or dispatch the call.

metrics.source_section: Access, Section 12: What Good Looks Like

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative correctly assigns the final call outcome (Book, Escalate Before Booking, Callback/Manual Scheduling, Dispatch Review, or Refer Out) based on the parameters of the job, strictly adhering to 'Do Not Book' hard rules.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the final disposition of the call to verify the CSR selected the correct outcome according to defined rules and the Electrical and Roofing Decisioning Matrices. Ensure the CSR never books prohibited scenarios without manager approval (e.g., refused fees, no decision-maker or authorization, no access, active safety hazards like flooding/fire risk, outside scope work, or repair requests for 16+ year old roofs).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Flawlessly selects the exact correct booking outcome based on scope, safety, decision-maker availability, and routing requirements, perfectly adhering to all hard rules.
evaluation_criteria.good: Selects an appropriate outcome but may miss a minor nuance, such as booking a job that should have technically been flagged for Dispatch Review.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Selects an incorrect outcome that causes scheduling friction, such as failing to escalate a call that required manager review before booking.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Violates a 'Do Not Book' hard rule by direct-booking a prohibited scenario without manager approval (e.g., unsafe job, refused fee, no access, out-of-scope, or 16+ year roof repair).

metrics.source_section: Section 3: Booking Outcomes & Section 6: Do Not Book — Hard Rules

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative accurately documents the customer's specific issue in their own words within the job summary.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the job summary notes in the booking system to verify that the customer's specific issue is recorded accurately and uses the customer's own words as required.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Perfectly captures the customer's issue in their own words, providing a clear and precise job summary.
evaluation_criteria.good: Captures the issue well, mostly using the customer's words with minor omissions.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Summary is vague, incomplete, or heavily paraphrased, losing key context of the customer's actual complaint.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to document the specific issue, or the notes are entirely inaccurate or missing.

metrics.source_section: Section 4. Job Summary, Section 12: What Good Looks Like

metrics.description: Measures compliance with the After-Hours and Emergency Protocol, including correct booking, dispatcher notification, safety advice, and surcharge application.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the call and booking system to ensure the CSR books emergencies appropriately (priority/emergency for electrical; inspection/emergency tarp for roofing), immediately notifies the on-call dispatcher, and gives required safety advice (e.g., turning off the main breaker if safe for electrical, moving valuables and placing containers for active roofing leaks). Verify the CSR correctly applies and quotes the after-hours surcharge ($149 for electrical, $199 for roofing tarp call-out) or waives it for club members.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Perfectly follows the emergency protocol, including proper booking, immediate dispatch notification, precise safety advice, and correct surcharge quoting or waiving.
evaluation_criteria.good: Follows the core emergency protocol (booking and dispatch notification) but misses a minor step, such as forgetting to advise moving valuables or missing a detail on the fee explanation.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Fails to provide crucial safety advice (like advising to turn off the breaker) or fails to accurately quote the after-hours surcharge.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to notify the on-call dispatcher, books the emergency incorrectly, or entirely ignores safety and fee protocols.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR adheres to the policy of not providing pricing over the phone.
metrics.evaluation_method: Listen to the call to ensure the CSR does not quote repair prices or give unauthorized estimates over the phone, properly deferring to the technician or estimator to provide options on-site.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Firmly and politely avoids giving pricing over the phone, correctly setting the expectation that pricing is provided on-site.
evaluation_criteria.good: Avoids giving pricing but the deflection or explanation might be slightly awkward or hesitant.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Provides vague price ranges or hints at costs that could set an incorrect expectation.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Violates policy by providing specific pricing for repairs or services over the phone.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative accurately diagnoses the issue, presents options clearly, and provides the necessary information for the customer to make a confident decision.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate customer interactions and job notes to determine if the diagnosis was accurate and if multiple options were presented clearly.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Accurately diagnoses the issue and presents clear options, acting as a trusted advisor to help the customer make a confident decision.
evaluation_criteria.good: Diagnoses the issue and presents options, but lacks slight clarity or misses minor details.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Diagnosis is unclear or options are presented confusingly, leaving the customer unsure.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to diagnose accurately or does not present options to the customer.

metrics.description: Ensures that every visit results in a sold repair, a sold replacement/upgrade, or a properly documented qualified reason if neither occurred on an OP-tagged job.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review CRM or job tickets for signed work orders or detailed documentation explaining why an OP-tagged job did not result in a sale.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Job ends in a sold repair/upgrade, or detailed and highly qualified reasons are documented for why no signed work order was achieved on an OP-tagged job.
evaluation_criteria.good: Job ends in a sale or a basic qualified reason is documented for no sale.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Job ends without a sale and the documented reason is vague or unconvincing (e.g., 'nothing to sell').
evaluation_criteria.poor: Leaves an OP-tagged job without a signed work order and fails to document any reason.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative reviews all critical job ticket information in the system (ServiceTitan) before leaving for the call, including customer details, job type, opportunity status, membership status, safety flags, and prior history.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate via ride-alongs, call debriefs, or reviewing whether the representative asks the customer for information that was already provided in the CSR notes or job ticket.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently reviews all 7 key elements of the job ticket before arrival and seamlessly integrates this prior knowledge into the customer interaction.
evaluation_criteria.good: Reviews the job ticket and grasps core details (job type, issue), but occasionally misses secondary context like prior call history or specific access notes.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Frequently arrives lacking crucial context such as club membership status or safety flags, leading to a disjointed customer experience.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to review the job ticket before arriving, routinely asking the customer to repeat information already documented by the CSR.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative properly introduces themselves by name and company, and confirms they are speaking with the decision-maker before proceeding with the call.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate via ride-alongs, call recordings, or customer feedback to ensure the representative follows the introduction script, confirms the decision-maker, and contacts the office if the decision-maker is absent.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Flawlessly introduces self by name and company, verifies the decision-maker immediately, and strictly follows protocol to call the office and halt diagnosis if the decision-maker is absent.
evaluation_criteria.good: Introduces self and verifies the decision-maker, but may occasionally deviate slightly from the preferred script or phrasing.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Forgets to state company name or assumes the person at the door is the decision-maker without explicitly confirming.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to introduce self professionally, does not confirm the decision-maker, or proceeds to diagnose/present options to a non-decision-maker.

metrics.description: Evaluates the representative's ability to ask the four key walk-in questions and actively listen to the customer before touching any equipment or diagnosing the issue.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate through ride-alongs or recorded interactions to observe if the rep asks about issue history, prior interventions, and secondary opportunities, and listens more than they talk.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently asks all four key walk-in questions before touching anything, actively listens, and allows the customer to fully explain and show the problem.
evaluation_criteria.good: Asks the majority of the walk-in questions and listens well, but may occasionally skip a secondary question (e.g., related issues in the home).
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Rushes the discovery process, talks more than listens, or begins interacting with equipment before the customer has finished explaining.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to ask the required discovery questions, ignores customer context, or immediately starts inspecting without letting the customer explain.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative checks for any active safety hazards (such as a burning smell, exposed wiring, or water near the panel) before proceeding with the job or assessment.
metrics.evaluation_method: Assess through ride-alongs, safety audits, or completion of job safety checklists to confirm that a visual and sensory hazard check is conducted prior to beginning work.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently and proactively conducts a thorough check for active hazards (smell, exposed wiring, water) before touching any equipment or proceeding with the job.
evaluation_criteria.good: Performs a general safety check before proceeding, but might occasionally need a reminder to check for specific environmental hazards.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Frequently rushes into the assessment without explicitly checking for hazards like water near panels or exposed wiring.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to perform any initial safety checks for active hazards, potentially putting themselves or the customer at risk.

metrics.source_section: Section: 1. Safety first: Check for any active hazards before proceeding

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative documents the initial state of the equipment by taking photos of the panel, affected circuits, and any visible damage before touching anything.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review job notes and CRM attachments to confirm that clear photos of the panel, affected circuits, and visible damage were taken and uploaded prior to the start of any physical work.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently captures clear, comprehensive photos of the panel, affected circuits, and all visible damage before touching any equipment.
evaluation_criteria.good: Captures necessary photos of the panel and damage before work, but may occasionally miss a specific affected circuit or take slightly unclear photos.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Frequently forgets to take photos before starting work, or captures incomplete/poor-quality documentation of the damage.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to take photos of the panel, circuits, or damage before touching the equipment.

metrics.source_section: Section: 2. Document before touching: Take photos of the panel, affected circuits, and any visible damage

metrics.description: Evaluates the representative's thoroughness in inspecting the electrical panel, including checking the age, brand, capacity versus current load, and the condition of breakers, bus bars, and wiring. Also measures strict adherence to mandatory disclosure and escalation protocols for specific hazardous setups (Federal Pacific, Zinsco, aluminum wiring).
metrics.evaluation_method: Review job notes, technical documentation, and escalation logs to ensure panel details (age, brand, capacity, condition) are recorded and verify that high-risk setups were properly disclosed to the customer and escalated.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Thoroughly inspects and documents panel age, brand, capacity vs. load, and component condition. Flawlessly identifies, immediately discloses, and escalates high-risk setups (Federal Pacific, Zinsco, aluminum wiring).
evaluation_criteria.good: Inspects and documents the panel effectively and catches hazardous setups, but documentation may lack minor details regarding the specific condition of bus bars or wiring.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Inspects the panel but fails to document capacity vs. load, poorly documents the condition of internal components, or is slow to disclose/escalate hazardous brands.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to conduct a thorough panel inspection, or completely misses, ignores, or fails to disclose and escalate known hazardous setups like Federal Pacific, Zinsco, or aluminum wiring.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative performs a full panel condition assessment and actively looks for upgrade opportunities on L1 calls (panels 10+ years old), rather than only addressing the reported issue.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review job documentation, CRM notes, and proposed options to verify that a full panel assessment was completed and upgrade options were explored for panels 10+ years old.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently assesses the full panel condition on L1 calls, moving beyond the reported issue to successfully identify, document, and present upgrade opportunities.
evaluation_criteria.good: Usually assesses the full panel condition on older panels and looks for upgrades, but documentation or presentation of the upgrade opportunity may occasionally lack detail.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Often focuses solely on the reported issue during L1 calls, frequently missing the requirement to thoroughly assess the full panel and look for upgrades.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to conduct a full panel assessment on L1 calls, completely ignoring the panel's age and missing obvious upgrade opportunities by only treating the reported symptom.

metrics.source_section: Section: 5. If this is an L1 call (10+ yr panel)

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative comprehensively documents their diagnostic findings, including photos, meter readings, and written notes in ServiceTitan, before presenting options to the customer.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review ServiceTitan job records to verify that required documentation (photos, meter readings, and written notes) is completed and logged prior to the presentation of options.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently and thoroughly documents all findings with clear photos, accurate meter readings, and detailed written notes in ServiceTitan before presenting any options.
evaluation_criteria.good: Documents findings in ServiceTitan before presenting options, but may occasionally miss a specific meter reading or provide slightly brief written notes.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Frequently presents options before fully completing the required documentation (photos, readings, notes) in ServiceTitan.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to document findings, take meter readings, or write notes in ServiceTitan before presenting options.

metrics.source_section: Section: 6. Document findings: Photo, meter readings, and written notes in ServiceTitan before presenting options

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative conducts a ground-level walkthrough prior to further inspection, specifically looking for and identifying visible sagging, missing material, damaged fascia, and gutter condition.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review job notes, photos, and CRM documentation to verify that observations from the initial ground-level walkthrough (including notes on sagging, missing materials, fascia, and gutters) were recorded.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently conducts a thorough ground-level walkthrough first, accurately identifying and documenting visible sagging, missing material, damaged fascia, and gutter conditions.
evaluation_criteria.good: Performs the ground-level walkthrough and notes major issues, but may occasionally miss minor details regarding fascia or gutter condition.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Frequently rushes or skips parts of the ground-level walkthrough, failing to note obvious visible exterior issues like sagging or damaged fascia.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to perform a ground-level walkthrough first or completely ignores visible exterior damage such as missing material and sagging.

metrics.source_section: Section: 7. Ground-level walkthrough first: Look for visible sagging, missing material, damaged fascia, gutter condition

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative thoroughly checks critical roof penetration points and surface wear, specifically inspecting flashing around chimneys, vents, skylights, and walls, as well as checking for underlayment exposure, granule loss, and blistering or lifting.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review job notes, inspection checklists, and attached photos to verify that flashing conditions and specific surface issues (underlayment exposure, granule loss, blistering, lifting) were assessed and documented.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently and thoroughly inspects and documents all required areas: flashing (chimneys, vents, skylights, walls), underlayment exposure, granule loss, and blistering/lifting.
evaluation_criteria.good: Inspects and documents the general condition of flashing and surface wear, but may occasionally omit minor details such as a specific vent or minor granule loss.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Fails to consistently check all specified components, frequently missing checks on underlayment exposure or specific flashing areas.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to inspect or document the condition of flashing, underlayment exposure, or shingle wear (granule loss, blistering, lifting).

metrics.source_section: Section: 9. Check: Flashing around chimneys, vents, skylights, and walls; underlayment exposure; granule loss; blistering or lifting

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative conducts an attic check (if accessible) to look for signs of roof leaks or structural issues, specifically checking for moisture staining, daylight showing through the roof, and compromised decking.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review job notes, inspection checklists, and attached photos to verify that the attic was inspected (or noted as inaccessible) and specific conditions (moisture staining, daylight, compromised decking) were properly assessed and documented.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently checks the attic (when accessible) and thoroughly documents the presence or absence of moisture staining, daylight, and compromised decking with detailed notes and photos.
evaluation_criteria.good: Conducts the attic check and documents findings, but may occasionally lack detail or omit photos of specific conditions.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Occasionally skips the attic check when it is accessible, or fails to specifically document checks for moisture, daylight, or decking issues.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to perform the attic check when accessible, or completely ignores obvious signs of moisture staining, daylight, or compromised decking.

metrics.source_section: Section: 10. Attic check (if accessible): Look for moisture staining, daylight, or compromised decking

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative confirms and documents the roof age, strictly adheres to the rule of providing only replacement estimates (no repair quotes) for roofs aged 16 years or older, and professionally communicates why repairs on a compromised base cannot be warrantied.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review job documentation, CRM records, and presented options to verify that roof age is explicitly documented and no repair quotes are provided for roofs 16+ years old. Assess call recordings or ride-alongs to verify proper use of the objection handling script explaining the compromised base.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently confirms and documents roof age. Flawlessly adheres to the rule by providing only replacement estimates, and professionally handles repair requests by clearly explaining the compromised base and warranty limitations using the approved script.
evaluation_criteria.good: Documents roof age and adheres to providing replacement estimates for older roofs. Explains why repairs can't be done, but may occasionally deviate from the preferred script or phrasing.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Fails to consistently document roof age, occasionally offers repair quotes on roofs that are 16+ years old, or struggles to clearly explain to the customer why a repair cannot be performed or warrantied.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Ignores the roof age rule entirely, routinely offering repair quotes on roofs older than 16 years, or argues with the customer when they request a repair.

metrics.source_section: Section: 12. Roof age rule: If roof is 16+ years — confirm and document. No repair quotes.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative captures a minimum of 10 photos per inspection to ensure the customer can see exactly what the representative saw.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review job documentation and CRM attachments to verify that at least 10 relevant photos from the inspection are present and were used to show the customer the findings.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently captures 10 or more high-quality photos per inspection, providing a comprehensive visual record to show the customer.
evaluation_criteria.good: Captures the required minimum of 10 photos, but some photos may occasionally lack clarity or clear context.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Frequently captures fewer than 10 photos per inspection, providing an incomplete visual record for the customer.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Captures very few or no photos, completely failing to visually document the inspection or show the customer what was found.

metrics.source_section: Section: 13. Photo everything: Minimum 10 photos per inspection.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative consistently presents three options to the customer. Providing only one option is strictly prohibited, and presenting exactly three options is non-negotiable on OP-tagged calls.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review presented estimates in the CRM to verify that three distinct options were built and presented to the customer on OP-tagged calls.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently builds and presents exactly three distinct options on OP-tagged calls, never offering just a single option.
evaluation_criteria.good: Generally presents three options on OP-tagged calls, but may occasionally present two; never presents just one option.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Occasionally presents only a single option on OP-tagged calls, violating the non-negotiable rule.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Frequently presents only one option to the customer, completely disregarding the non-negotiable three-option rule on OP-tagged calls.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative structures options into Good (immediate issue), Better (issue + related risks), and Best (full solution), presents them in Best-to-Good order, explains tradeoffs, and allows the customer to choose without pre-selecting.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate via ride-alongs, call recordings, or estimate reviews to verify the Good/Better/Best structure, top-down presentation order (Best first), explanation of tradeoffs, and absence of rep pre-selection.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently builds Good/Better/Best options accurately, always presents Best first, clearly explains tradeoffs, and allows the customer to choose without pre-selecting.
evaluation_criteria.good: Builds appropriate options and generally presents Best first, but may occasionally lack depth in explaining tradeoffs.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Builds options but presents them out of order (e.g., starts with Good), rushes through tradeoffs, or subtly pushes the customer toward a specific choice.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to structure options into Good/Better/Best, presents the cheapest option first, or visibly pre-selects the option for the customer.

metrics.metric_name: L1 Panel Upgrade Presentation & Objection Handling
metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative presents a panel upgrade as the 'Best' option on L1 calls (panels 10+ years old), includes specific value points (capacity, EV readiness, safety, permit/warranty), and professionally handles objections by validating the customer's choice and documenting findings.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate via ride-alongs or call recordings to verify the rep positions the upgrade as the Best option, hits the required value points, and respectfully handles objections (e.g., 'I just want to fix the one breaker') without pressure.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Flawlessly positions the panel upgrade as the Best option, clearly articulates all required value points (capacity, EV readiness, safety, permits/warranty), and professionally handles objections by validating the customer's choice and documenting findings for the future.
evaluation_criteria.good: Positions the upgrade as the Best option and covers most key value points. Handles objections politely, though may occasionally miss a minor detail like EV readiness or explicitly stating documentation intentions.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Presents the upgrade but fails to position it as the Best option, omits multiple key value points during the presentation, or struggles to handle the 'just fix the breaker' objection smoothly.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to present a panel upgrade option on an L1 call, argues with the customer during objections, or uses high-pressure tactics instead of validating their choice.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative checks for and presents secondary opportunities (e.g., surge protection, GFCI updates, EV charger interest) during L2/L3 calls, even when the primary system is younger and in good shape.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review ride-alongs, call recordings, or CRM estimates to confirm the representative diagnoses the primary issue first and utilizes the low-pressure script to offer secondary enhancements.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently diagnoses the primary issue first and seamlessly transitions to presenting relevant secondary opportunities (e.g., surge protection) using a natural, conversational script.
evaluation_criteria.good: Diagnoses the primary issue and usually offers secondary opportunities, but may occasionally miss minor updates or use a less effective transition.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Frequently focuses solely on the primary diagnosis, missing obvious secondary opportunities like surge protection or GFCI updates on newer systems.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Treats L2/L3 calls strictly as break-fix, completely failing to check for or present any secondary opportunities.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative consistently offers the Home Protection Plan (Club Membership) on every call where the customer is not already a member, hitting key value points such as price, annual inspections, discounts, and priority scheduling.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate via ride-alongs or call recordings to confirm the representative offers the membership to non-members during the wrap-up and includes the key benefits outlined in the script.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently offers the Home Protection Plan to non-members on every call, smoothly integrating it into the wrap-up and clearly stating all key benefits (price, inspections, discount, priority scheduling).
evaluation_criteria.good: Offers the membership to non-members, but may occasionally omit a minor benefit or deliver the pitch somewhat mechanically.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Inconsistently offers the membership to non-members or frequently fails to articulate the primary benefits and pricing.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails entirely to offer the Club Membership to non-members before wrapping up the call.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative effectively transitions from inspection to presentation on a repair call by showing photos of the issue on a tablet and offering three specifically structured options: Good (spot repair), Better (repair plus addressing secondary wear), and Best (full section replacement).
metrics.evaluation_method: Review ride-alongs, call recordings, or CRM estimates to verify the representative uses tablet photos to explain the source of the leak and builds the specific Good, Better, and Best options for roof repairs.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Seamlessly transitions by using tablet photos to show the exact damage (e.g., separated flashing) and clearly presents Good (spot repair), Better (repair + secondary wear), and Best (full section replacement to address root cause).
evaluation_criteria.good: Uses photos and presents the three options, but explanation of the root cause or the value difference between the Better and Best options may lack depth.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Presents options but forgets to show tablet photos to the customer during the transition, or improperly structures the Good/Better/Best options (e.g., omitting secondary wear).
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to show visual evidence of the issue on a tablet and does not offer a Good/Better/Best choice, such as quoting only a single spot repair.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative effectively transitions to a replacement estimate by summarizing visual findings, presenting material choices based on the existing system and budget, and explaining warranty terms, timeline/permit process, and payment options.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate via ride-alongs, call recordings, or CRM reviews to confirm the representative summarizes inspection findings and clearly presents two material choices, explaining the long-term benefits of each.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Seamlessly transitions by summarizing specific visual findings, presents appropriate material options (shingle, tile, foam), and thoroughly explains warranty terms, the timeline/permit process, and available payment options.
evaluation_criteria.good: Summarizes findings and presents material choices, but explanations regarding warranty, timelines, permit handling, or payment options may occasionally lack detail or clarity.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Fails to clearly summarize findings before presenting options, or misses key presentation elements such as warranty terms, timeline, permit process, or payment options.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to present suitable material options, or simply hands over a replacement quote without explaining the materials, warranties, timeline, or payment options.

metrics.description: Evaluates the representative's ability to professionally navigate common customer objections (budget, spouse, competitor quotes, delay, and minimum scope) using approved, low-pressure responses that emphasize transparency, accurate scope comparison, and risk documentation.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate via ride-alongs, call recordings, or customer feedback to verify the representative uses appropriate scripted responses for objections, effectively compares quote scopes, validates the customer's choice, and avoids high-pressure tactics.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Flawlessly handles common objections using the recommended frameworks (e.g., comparing apples-to-apples for cheaper quotes, explaining risks for delays), immediately accepts minimum option choices without pressure, and thoroughly documents findings.
evaluation_criteria.good: Professionally handles objections without pressure and validates the customer's choice, but may occasionally omit a specific detail (like explicitly mentioning permits/warranties during a competitor comparison) or slightly deviate from the preferred script.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Struggles to address objections effectively, forgets to offer email quotes for absent spouses, fails to explain risks when customers delay, or shows visible frustration when a customer chooses the minimum option.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Argues with the customer, uses high-pressure sales tactics when a customer wants to wait or choose the minimum, or fails to document risks when the customer delays.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative asks the final closing question directly, refrains from volunteering 'take your time' immediately after presenting options, and allows comfortable silence for the customer to process and respond.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate via call recordings or ride-alongs to verify that the representative asks a clear closing question, avoids immediately offering the customer an out (e.g., 'take your time'), and successfully maintains silence until the customer responds.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Asks the closing question confidently, strictly avoids saying 'take your time', and maintains comfortable silence until the customer speaks.
evaluation_criteria.good: Asks the closing question and waits, but may slightly break the silence a bit early or use slightly less direct closing phrasing.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Volunteers 'take your time' immediately after presenting options, undermining the decision process, or rushes to fill the silence too quickly.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to ask a clear closing question, actively talks over the customer's decision time, or completely avoids closing the sale.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative obtains a customer signature on the work order in ServiceTitan before starting any work, and explicitly confirms the scope of work, price, payment method, and start date.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review ServiceTitan records for timestamps showing the signature was obtained before work commenced, and verify through call recordings or ride-alongs that scope, price, and payment were confirmed.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently secures the signature in ServiceTitan before work begins and thoroughly confirms scope, price, payment method, and start date.
evaluation_criteria.good: Obtains the signature before work, but occasionally misses confirming a minor administrative detail verbally.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Frequently forgets to confirm key details like price or payment method verbally, or delays getting the signature until after work has begun.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Starts work without securing a customer signature or fails completely to confirm the scope and price with the customer beforehand.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative conducts a post-job walkthrough, shows before/after photos, asks if there are any other issues (electrical or roofing) on the customer's mind, and requests a Google review.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate via customer feedback, ride-alongs, or call recordings to confirm the representative walks the customer through the completed work, shows photos, asks about additional needs, and requests a review.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently walks the customer through the work, uses before/after photos, asks about other home issues, and professionally requests a Google review.
evaluation_criteria.good: Conducts the walkthrough and shows photos, but occasionally forgets to ask about secondary trade issues or skips the review request.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Frequently forgets to show before/after photos or completely skips the review request and cross-trade inquiry.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Leaves the job site without walking the customer through the completed work or attempting any post-job wrap-up steps.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative updates ServiceTitan with the final job status, closing notes, final photos, and next steps before leaving the property. For electrical calls, this includes panel details, fault confirmation, options/prices, and outcome. For roofing calls, this includes roof age, material, condition rating, areas inspected with flagged photos, leak source confirmation with moisture meter readings, all three options/prices presented, outcome (sold or specific reason if nothing sold), and a minimum of 10 photos.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review ServiceTitan job records to verify that job status, comprehensive notes (including specific electrical or roofing checklist items like panel/roof details, moisture/voltage meter readings, presented options, and outcome reasons), final photos, and next steps are logged prior to the representative's departure from the site.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Flawlessly updates ServiceTitan with all required elements before leaving every property. Rigorously includes trade-specific details (electrical panel/fault or roofing age/material/leak source), meter readings, options/prices, precise outcome reasons, and all required photos (minimum 10 for roofing).
evaluation_criteria.good: Updates the system before leaving, but occasionally provides brief notes, misses logging a minor next step, or omits a minor trade-specific checklist detail (e.g., specific meter reading or condition rating).
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Frequently leaves the property before completing system updates, or leaves notes/photos incomplete (e.g., missing options presented, vague outcome reasons, missing minimum photo counts).
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to update ServiceTitan with status, notes, or photos after completing the job, or completely ignores the required trade-specific documentation checklists.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative strictly avoids giving prices over the phone on follow-up calls and appropriately directs the customer to the office.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review call logs, office feedback, and communication records to confirm that follow-up pricing inquiries are handled directly by the office, not by the field rep over the phone.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Flawlessly defers all phone pricing questions on follow-ups to the office without quoting numbers, setting professional boundaries.
evaluation_criteria.good: Defers pricing to the office, though might occasionally use soft, non-binding conversational language before doing so.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Partially discusses or hints at pricing over the phone before finally directing the customer to the office.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Violates company policy by giving a price over the phone on a follow-up call instead of directing it to the office.

metrics.source_section: Section 10: Behaviors That Are Never Acceptable

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative ensures all promised permit timelines are explicitly confirmed with the office before communicating them to the customer.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review job notes, customer agreements, and office communication logs to verify that any permit timelines promised to the customer were pre-vetted by the office.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently contacts the office to confirm exact permit timelines before promising any dates or schedules to the customer.
evaluation_criteria.good: Generally communicates accurate permit timelines, but may occasionally rely on standard historical estimates instead of immediate direct confirmation for routine jobs.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Frequently guesses permit timelines without checking with the office, leading to potential customer expectation issues.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Violates company policy by explicitly promising a permit timeline to the customer that has not been confirmed with the office.

metrics.source_section: Section 10: Behaviors That Are Never Acceptable

metrics.description: Measures the percentage of OP-tagged calls that result in a sold job.
metrics.evaluation_method: Calculate the percentage of OP-tagged calls resulting in sold work via CRM reporting.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Conversion rate is consistently well above the 60% target.
evaluation_criteria.good: Conversion rate meets or slightly exceeds the 60% target.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Conversion rate frequently falls slightly below the 60% target.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Conversion rate is consistently and significantly below 60%.

metrics.evaluation_method: Calculate the average ticket value for electrical service jobs via CRM reporting.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Average electrical job value is consistently well above $450.
evaluation_criteria.good: Average electrical job value meets or slightly exceeds the $450 target.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Average electrical job value frequently dips below the $450 target.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Average electrical job value is consistently and significantly below $450.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Average roofing job value is consistently well above $2,500.
evaluation_criteria.good: Average roofing job value meets or slightly exceeds the $2,500 target.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Average roofing job value frequently dips below the $2,500 target.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Average roofing job value is consistently and significantly below $2,500.

metrics.description: Measures customer satisfaction based on ratings gathered from post-job surveys.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review post-job survey scores and customer feedback logs.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently achieves customer satisfaction scores of 4.9 to 5.0.
evaluation_criteria.good: Maintains a customer satisfaction score that meets or slightly exceeds the 4.7 target.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Customer satisfaction score frequently dips below the 4.7 threshold.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Customer satisfaction score is consistently poor (e.g., below 4.0) or generates frequent customer complaints.

Jada clarified that HOAs typically require the homeowner to initiate contact, and Robert agreed to reach out to them. Jada offered to provide any necessary information for the HOA and advised that the current schedule might need adjustment if the approval process is delayed.

The property is in an HOA community, and Robert still needs to obtain approval for the roofing project.
Robert thought Orlando was handling the HOA communication, but Jada explained that HOAs usually only speak directly with homeowners.
The scheduled installation dates are contingent on HOA approval and may need to be moved if the process takes too long.
