metrics.description: Measures the CSR's ability to qualify the call correctly, book it to the right service type, and tag it accurately.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate call and system records to verify that the customer issue matches the assigned service type and tags based on the Electrical and Roofing Decisioning Matrices (e.g., booking an L1 service call for 10+ year old panels, booking an estimate rather than repair for a 16+ year old roof). Verify correct application of Opportunity (OP) or No Opportunity (NO) tags. Ensure exactly all 4 electrical tags OR all 5 roofing tags are correctly applied (not defaulted without checking). Ensure optional situational tags are applied as needed, and that the Job Summary strictly captures the customer's actual words rather than the CSR's interpretation.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Call is correctly qualified, booked to the exact right service type, and tagged completely accurately.
evaluation_criteria.good: Call is qualified and booked correctly, with only minor inaccuracies in tagging.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Call qualification is incomplete, leading to borderline incorrect service type or missing tags.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Call is booked wrong (incorrect service type or poor qualification) resulting in a wasted tech visit, customer experience failure, and lost opportunity.

metrics.source_section: Version 1.0 | Customer Service Representatives, Section 12: What Good Looks Like

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR sets the customer's expectations appropriately before concluding the call.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the end of the call to ensure expectations regarding the service visit were clearly communicated. Specifically, verify that the CSR verbally confirms the appointment window (e.g., 3-hour window), explains the fee waiver policy, and informs the customer they will receive a text with the technician's name and photo. For electrical estimates, verify the CSR sets the expectation that the technician will walk the customer through all options and pricing on-site and that the company handles the permit process. For roofing estimates, verify the CSR sets the expectation that the estimator will bring everything needed for a full inspection and requests the customer's solar provider contact info if applicable.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Customer expectations are clearly and fully set before the CSR hangs up.
evaluation_criteria.good: Most expectations are set, but some details could be clearer.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Expectations are rushed or vague, leaving the customer potentially confused.
evaluation_criteria.poor: CSR completely fails to set customer expectations before hanging up.

metrics.source_section: Version 1.0 | Customer Service Representatives, Section 12: What Good Looks Like

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR uses the complete, approved company greeting and provides their name when answering a call.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the beginning of the call to verify the CSR uses the exact greeting: 'Thank you for calling Apex Power and Roofing. This is [Your Name], how can I help you today?'

evaluation_criteria.excellent: CSR uses the exact full greeting and clearly states their name.
evaluation_criteria.good: CSR uses a professional greeting that includes the full company name and their own name, with only minor deviations from the script.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: CSR uses a partial greeting, rushing through it or missing a component like 'how can I help you today?'.
evaluation_criteria.poor: CSR answers with just 'Hello' or 'Apex Power', or completely fails to provide their name.

metrics.description: Measures the CSR's ability to properly verify existing customer profiles or completely capture required information for new customers.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review call recordings and system entries to verify the CSR confirms address and checks membership/revenue history for existing customers, or captures name, address, callback number (specifically confirming the callback number is correct in ServiceTitan), email, and homeowner status for new customers.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: CSR perfectly verifies existing profile (address confirmation, membership, revenue) or completely captures new customer details (name, address, callback number, email, and homeowner status).
evaluation_criteria.good: CSR captures or verifies the primary information but misses one minor element like capturing an email address or checking past revenue history.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: CSR misses multiple required fields, fails to ask if the caller is the homeowner, or fails to check club membership status.
evaluation_criteria.poor: CSR completely fails to verify the customer profile, enters the wrong service address, or skips the customer information capture process.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR asks the appropriate diagnostic and safety questions to fully understand the customer's issue, and correctly identifies and escalates critical safety hazards.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the call to verify the CSR asks key problem-assessment questions, including specific situational questions from the Electrical and Roofing Decisioning Matrices (e.g., confirming panel/roof age, isolated vs. widespread issues, roof type, interior damage, storm timing, and solar presence). For electrical calls, verify that severe hazards (burning smell, sparks, shock, smoke, or active flooding near panel) are immediately flagged as emergencies or escalated.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: CSR thoroughly assesses the issue by asking all required situational questions (e.g., panel age, circuit load, roof age, roof type, solar presence) and correctly flags any severe safety hazards (e.g., burning smell, sparks, shock, smoke, active flooding) as an immediate emergency, halting standard booking.
evaluation_criteria.good: CSR asks what is happening and checks for major issues, but may miss a specific detail like exactly when it started, system partial/full status, or storm timing.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: CSR gathers only basic details about the problem and completely fails to ask about safety concerns, leaking, or sparking.
evaluation_criteria.poor: CSR fails to ask diagnostic questions, making no effort to understand the specific problem, or critically fails to flag severe safety hazards (sparks, smoke, shock, active flooding) as an immediate emergency.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR asks the necessary questions to confirm that the decision-maker will be present, authorized to approve work, and ready to move forward with repairs during the appointment.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the call to verify the CSR asks if the customer will be home, if anyone else is involved in approving repairs, and if they are ready to move forward with a solution. For tenants, verify the CSR confirms who pays and authorizes, escalating if unclear.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: CSR effectively asks all necessary questions to confirm the customer's presence, approval authority, and readiness to move forward.
evaluation_criteria.good: CSR asks if the customer will be home and checks for approval authority, but misses asking if they are ready to move forward.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: CSR only asks if the customer will be home, failing to inquire about approval authority or readiness to proceed.
evaluation_criteria.poor: CSR completely fails to ask any questions regarding the decision-maker's presence or authority to approve repairs.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR asks the necessary questions to ensure safe and unimpeded access for the technician, including verifying an adult (18+) will be present, pets are secured, and trade-specific access requirements are met.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the call to confirm the CSR asks if someone 18 or older will be home, if pets are secured, and checks trade-specific access details (e.g., electrical panel/attic accessibility, or roofing gates/HOA/solar restrictions).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: CSR effectively asks all applicable access questions, including 18+ presence, securing pets, and all relevant trade-specific requirements.
evaluation_criteria.good: CSR asks the primary access questions (18+ and pets) but misses a trade-specific access detail.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: CSR asks only basic access questions but misses crucial elements like the 18+ requirement or securing pets.
evaluation_criteria.poor: CSR completely fails to ask any access-related questions before concluding the booking.

metrics.description: Measures the CSR's ability to correctly assign the call to one of the five designated outcomes (Book, Escalate Before Booking, Callback / Manual Scheduling, Dispatch Review, or Refer Out) based on job scope, safety, and constraints.
metrics.evaluation_method: Evaluate the final disposition of the call to verify it matches the correct outcome criteria per the Electrical and Roofing Decisioning Matrices (e.g., referring out appliance repair, low voltage, or roof pests; escalating if a customer refuses the diagnostic fee or tenant authorization is unclear; or correctly booking estimates vs. repairs based on roof age).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: CSR accurately selects the exact appropriate outcome from the five available options based on all gathered call constraints.
evaluation_criteria.good: CSR selects a generally appropriate outcome but might miss minor nuances, such as not utilizing dispatch review for a borderline complex job.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: CSR selects an incorrect outcome that causes minor scheduling friction or requires later correction by dispatch.
evaluation_criteria.poor: CSR selects a completely wrong outcome, such as directly booking an out-of-scope or unsafe job, or referring out a highly qualified and profitable call.

metrics.description: Measures whether the CSR correctly executes emergency procedures, including proper booking, dispatch notification, customer safety advice, and accurate surcharge quoting.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review calls involving electrical (sparks, shock, no power to whole home, burning smell) or roofing emergencies (active interior water damage, ceiling collapse risk). Verify the CSR books the call as an emergency/priority or inspection/emergency tarp, immediately notifies the on-call dispatcher, and provides the correct safety advice (turning off the main breaker if safe for electrical; moving valuables and placing containers under leaks for roofing). Additionally, verify the CSR correctly quotes the after-hours surcharge ($149 for electrical, $199 for roofing tarp call-out) and accurately waives it for club members.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: CSR executes all emergency steps flawlessly: books the emergency correctly, immediately notifies dispatch, provides specific safety/mitigation advice, and accurately quotes or waives the after-hours surcharge.
evaluation_criteria.good: CSR correctly books the emergency and notifies dispatch, but misses a minor detail such as failing to provide mitigation advice (e.g., moving valuables) or hesitating on the surcharge rules.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: CSR identifies the emergency but fails to notify the on-call dispatcher promptly, or fails to mention the after-hours surcharge entirely.
evaluation_criteria.poor: CSR fails to follow emergency protocols, does not notify dispatch, provides unsafe advice, or completely fails to advise the customer of the correct fees or safety steps.

metrics.description: Measures the CSR's ability to effectively use approved scripts for common objections and specific customer scenarios, including requests for pricing over the phone, home warranties, non-urgent inquiries, and club member acknowledgment.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the call to verify the CSR uses standard scripts when applicable: refusing to guess prices over the phone while clearly explaining the $89 diagnostic fee (and that it is waived if work is approved); explaining to home warranty customers that they are not billed directly but will receive a detailed report for reimbursement; attempting to schedule non-urgent callers by offering available times and free estimates; explaining the permit process is fully handled by the company; and immediately acknowledging Club Members by explicitly highlighting their priority scheduling and waived after-hours fees.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: CSR flawlessly identifies the scenario and delivers the approved script confidently, hitting all key points without hesitation (e.g., diagnostic fee structure, club benefits, home warranty reimbursement).
evaluation_criteria.good: CSR delivers the correct general response for the scenario but misses minor script details (e.g., forgets to explicitly mention the diagnostic fee is waived if work is approved, or misses a specific detail about home warranty reimbursement).
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: CSR handles the scenario poorly, hesitates, or misses major components of the required script, such as failing to acknowledge a club member's specific benefits immediately.
evaluation_criteria.poor: CSR violates the core rules of the scenario, such as attempting to quote an exact price over the phone for repairs, flatly turning away a home warranty or non-urgent customer without offering the standard solution, or completely ignoring a caller's club membership status.

metrics.description: Measures the representative's ability to act as a trusted advisor by diagnosing the customer's home issues accurately.
metrics.evaluation_method: Assess whether the rep effectively identified the root cause and positioned themselves as an advisor rather than just fixing a surface-level problem.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Diagnoses accurately, addresses root causes, and clearly establishes a trusted advisor relationship.
evaluation_criteria.good: Diagnoses the issue accurately and advises the customer properly.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Diagnoses the issue but fails to act as an advisor or misses underlying problems.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to diagnose accurately or acts only as an order-taker fixing the immediate problem.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the rep presents options clearly and provides the necessary information for the customer to make a confident decision.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review interaction to ensure the customer is given clear repair and replacement/upgrade options with sufficient detail. Verify the rep uses collaborative transition scripting to empower the customer (e.g., 'Let me walk you through your options so you can decide what makes the most sense...').

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Presents multiple clear options (repair vs. upgrade) and provides comprehensive information that strongly empowers a confident customer decision.
evaluation_criteria.good: Presents options clearly and provides sufficient information for a decision.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Presents options but lacks clarity or fails to provide enough information for the customer to feel confident.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to present options or confuses the customer with unclear information.

metrics.description: Ensures every visit (especially OP-tagged jobs) results in either a signed work order (sold repair or replacement/upgrade) or a documented qualified reason for no sale.
metrics.evaluation_method: Audit OP-tagged job tickets to verify outcome. Check for a signed work order detailing what was sold and why, or a clear, qualified written reason if no sale was made (e.g., customer declined, decision-maker absent, price objection).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Always achieves a sold repair/replacement (documenting what was sold and why) or provides exceptionally detailed, valid documentation for why a work order wasn't signed (e.g., specific price objection, decision-maker absent).
evaluation_criteria.good: Consistently achieves a sale or provides a qualified specific reason for no sale on OP-tagged jobs.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Occasionally leaves jobs without a sale and provides vague or incomplete documentation as to why.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Labels OP-tagged jobs as 'nothing to sell' or leaves without a signed work order and fails to document a specific, qualified reason.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative reviews and confirms all necessary customer and job details in ServiceTitan before leaving for the call.
metrics.evaluation_method: Verify that the rep checks customer details, job type/level, opportunity status, club membership, CSR notes, safety flags, and prior history before arrival.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently and thoroughly reviews all job ticket details, history, and safety flags before leaving, using the information to effectively plan the approach.
evaluation_criteria.good: Reviews the job ticket and confirms essential details (name, job type, status, notes) before arriving.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Reviews some details but misses key information such as prior call history, membership status, or safety flags.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to review the job ticket before arrival, arriving unprepared for the specific customer or job context.

metrics.description: Ensures the representative arrives with all necessary tools, documentation, and specific equipment required for the job based on prior notes.
metrics.evaluation_method: Verify the rep has the required equipment upon arrival, including a camera/tablet, moisture meter, inspection checklist, estimate forms, and the appropriate ladder based on CSR notes.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Always arrives fully equipped with all standard tools, necessary forms, and specifically tailored equipment (like correct ladder size) based on job notes.
evaluation_criteria.good: Arrives with the standard required equipment to successfully perform the inspection and diagnosis.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Occasionally forgets minor tools or forms, requiring workarounds but not stopping the job.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Arrives missing critical tools (e.g., ladder, moisture meter) or forms, significantly delaying or hindering the job.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative correctly introduces themselves and ensures the decision-maker is present before proceeding with diagnosis or options presentation.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the initial door interaction to check if the rep stated their name and company, confirmed they are speaking with the decision-maker, and halted diagnosis/presentation (calling the office instead) if the decision-maker was absent.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently delivers a professional, fully-scripted introduction, strictly confirms decision-maker presence, and perfectly follows protocol if they are absent.
evaluation_criteria.good: Introduces themselves by name and company and confirms the decision-maker before proceeding.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Forgets to state company name or starts inspecting without fully confirming if the person present is the decision-maker.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to introduce themselves properly or proceeds to diagnose and present options when the decision-maker is not home.

metrics.description: Measures the representative's ability to ask the right questions and actively listen to the customer before physically assessing the problem.
metrics.evaluation_method: Monitor the first two minutes of the interaction to ensure the rep asks open-ended walk-in questions (about experience, timeline, previous inspections, and secondary issues) and allows the customer to explain fully without interruption.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Asks all key walk-in questions, actively listens, uncovers secondary opportunities, and fully refrains from diagnosing until the customer finishes explaining.
evaluation_criteria.good: Asks required discovery questions and listens to the customer explain the problem before touching anything.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Rushes through discovery questions, interrupts the customer, or starts physically assessing the problem before listening to the full explanation.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to ask discovery questions, completely ignores the customer's explanation, or forms an opinion before hearing the customer out.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative checks the work area for active safety hazards before proceeding with the inspection or diagnosis.
metrics.evaluation_method: Verify that the representative performs a preliminary safety check for active hazards such as a burning smell, exposed wiring, or water near electrical panels before proceeding.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Proactively and thoroughly inspects the work area for all potential active hazards, strictly adhering to safety protocols before proceeding.
evaluation_criteria.good: Checks for active safety hazards (e.g., burning smell, exposed wiring, water) before proceeding with the job.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Performs a cursory or incomplete safety check, potentially overlooking minor hazards.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to perform a safety check, ignoring potential hazards like exposed wiring or water near panels, and proceeds recklessly.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative documents the initial state of the work area by taking photos of the panel, affected circuits, and any visible damage prior to physically touching or altering the equipment.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the job ticket or documentation system to verify that pre-work photos (showing the panel, affected circuits, and visible damage) were taken and uploaded before any physical work or manipulation began.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Takes highly clear, comprehensive photos of all relevant components (panel, circuits, visible damage) from multiple angles before touching anything, and properly logs them in the system.
evaluation_criteria.good: Takes necessary photos of the panel, affected circuits, and visible damage before touching or altering them.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Takes photos but misses key components (e.g., captures damage but not the broader panel context) or takes them after making minor physical alterations.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to take photos before touching, moving, or altering the equipment, completely missing the pre-work documentation step.

metrics.description: Evaluates the representative's thoroughness in inspecting the electrical panel (age, brand, capacity, condition), ensuring that for L1 calls (10+ yr panels) a full assessment is performed beyond the reported issue to identify upgrade opportunities.
metrics.evaluation_method: Verify that the rep assesses the panel's age, brand, capacity vs. load, and condition. For L1 calls, ensure the rep assesses the full panel condition rather than just the reported issue, actively looking for upgrade opportunities. Check that high-risk brands or aluminum wiring are properly disclosed and escalated.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Thoroughly inspects and documents all panel details, flawlessly executes disclosure protocols for hazards, and conducts comprehensive full-panel assessments on L1 calls to proactively identify valid upgrade opportunities.
evaluation_criteria.good: Inspects the panel's age, brand, capacity, and condition, escalates known hazards, and assesses the full panel for upgrade opportunities on L1 calls (10+ yr panels) rather than just the reported issue.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Inspects the panel but misses checking or documenting core areas, or fails to look beyond the immediate reported issue on an L1 call.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to properly inspect the panel, ignores critical component conditions, fails to escalate hazards, or solely focuses on the reported issue on L1 calls without assessing overall panel condition.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative documents all diagnostic findings, including photos, meter readings, and written notes in ServiceTitan, before presenting options to the customer.
metrics.evaluation_method: Verify in ServiceTitan that photos, meter readings, and written diagnostic notes were uploaded and saved prior to the time options were presented to the customer.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently and meticulously documents high-quality photos, precise meter readings, and highly detailed written notes in ServiceTitan before ever presenting options.
evaluation_criteria.good: Documents necessary photos, meter readings, and written notes in ServiceTitan before presenting options.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Documents some findings but misses key elements like meter readings or detailed notes, or completes the documentation during or after presenting options.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to document findings, photos, or meter readings in ServiceTitan prior to presenting options to the customer.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative performs an initial ground-level walkthrough to identify visible exterior damage before proceeding with a closer or elevated inspection.
metrics.evaluation_method: Verify that the representative conducts a ground-level visual inspection specifically looking for visible sagging, missing material, damaged fascia, and overall gutter condition.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Conducts a highly thorough ground-level walkthrough, proactively identifying and documenting all visible sagging, missing material, damaged fascia, and gutter conditions.
evaluation_criteria.good: Conducts a ground-level walkthrough to check for visible sagging, missing material, damaged fascia, and gutter condition.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Performs a rushed ground-level check, missing obvious visible issues with the roofline, materials, fascia, or gutters.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to perform a ground-level walkthrough entirely, missing visible exterior issues before proceeding to the next step.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative thoroughly inspects specific vulnerable roof components, including flashing, underlayment, granule condition, and shingle integrity.
metrics.evaluation_method: Verify through documentation or job notes that the representative explicitly checked flashing (around chimneys, vents, skylights, and walls), underlayment exposure, granule loss, and signs of blistering or lifting.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Meticulously checks and documents the condition of all critical components (all penetration flashings, underlayment, granules, blistering/lifting) with clear evidence.
evaluation_criteria.good: Inspects for flashing issues around penetrations and walls, underlayment exposure, granule loss, and blistering or lifting.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Inspects the roof but misses checking or documenting key vulnerable areas like specific flashings, underlayment, or granule loss.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to inspect critical roof components or completely ignores clear signs of granule loss, blistering, lifting, or flashing damage.

metrics.source_section: Section: 9. Check: Flashing around chimneys, vents, skylights, and walls; underlayment exposure;

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative performs an internal inspection of the attic (if accessible) to identify underlying roof damage.
metrics.evaluation_method: Verify through documentation or job notes that the representative checked the attic for moisture staining, visible daylight, and compromised decking.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Thoroughly inspects and documents the attic space (when accessible) with clear evidence (e.g., photos, notes) regarding moisture staining, daylight, and decking integrity.
evaluation_criteria.good: Inspects the accessible attic space for moisture staining, daylight, and compromised decking.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Checks the attic but fails to fully assess or document key indicators like minor moisture staining or visible daylight.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to inspect an accessible attic, missing critical internal signs of roof damage.

metrics.description: Ensures the representative correctly identifies roofs that are 16+ years old, restricts quoting to replacement estimates only, and effectively handles repair requests by explaining structural and warranty limitations.
metrics.evaluation_method: Verify roof age is confirmed and documented. If 16+ years old, check that no repair quotes were offered. Monitor interaction to ensure that if a repair is requested, the rep uses the script explaining that the roof reached its service life end, the base is compromised, and repairs cannot be warrantied, offering only a full replacement quote.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Proactively confirms roof age, strictly limits quotes to replacements for 16+ year roofs, and flawlessly uses the script to empathetically explain the compromised base and warranty limitations when redirecting repair requests.
evaluation_criteria.good: Confirms roof age, adheres to providing only replacement estimates for 16+ year roofs, and adequately explains why repairs cannot be performed or warrantied on the compromised base.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Follows the 16+ year rule but fails to clearly communicate the structural and warranty reasons (compromised base) to the customer when a repair is requested.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Quotes a repair on a roof that is 16+ years old, violating the rule, or refuses a repair request without providing the proper structural and warranty explanation.

metrics.description: Ensures the representative takes a minimum of 10 photos per inspection and uses them to visually show the customer the findings.
metrics.evaluation_method: Verify through job documentation that a minimum of 10 photos were filed (including a photo for each inspected area flagged), and review interaction notes to confirm these photos were shown to the customer to illustrate the findings.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Takes significantly more than the minimum 10 photos, capturing comprehensive, high-quality details, and highly effectively uses them to visually guide the customer through the findings.
evaluation_criteria.good: Takes a minimum of 10 photos per inspection and successfully shows the customer exactly what was seen.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Takes photos but fails to meet the minimum of 10, or takes the required photos but does not clearly present them to the customer.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Takes few to no photos, severely failing the minimum requirement, and provides no visual evidence to the customer.

metrics.description: Ensures the representative always presents three options to the customer, enforcing this as a non-negotiable standard on OP-tagged calls.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the job ticket and estimate documentation on OP-tagged calls to verify that exactly three distinct options were built and presented to the customer.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently builds and presents three distinct, well-crafted options on all calls, flawlessly executing the non-negotiable rule on OP-tagged jobs.
evaluation_criteria.good: Presents three options on OP-tagged calls as required by policy.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Presents multiple options but occasionally falls short of providing the full three options on OP-tagged calls.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Presents only one option to the customer on an OP-tagged call, directly violating the non-negotiable policy.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative effectively presents a panel upgrade as the 'Best' option on L1 calls (10+ year old panels), including key value drivers and properly handling repair-only objections.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review interaction on L1 Panel Calls (OP-tagged) to verify the rep presents the panel upgrade as the 'Best' option. Ensure the presentation includes current capacity vs. modern demands, EV charger readiness (if applicable), safety improvements, and permit/warranty details. Verify the rep handles 'repair only' objections by validating the customer's choice while leaving the documented upgrade on file.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Perfectly presents the upgrade with all key points (capacity, EV, safety, permit/warranty) and handles repair-only objections flawlessly using the provided script without pressure.
evaluation_criteria.good: Presents the panel upgrade as the Best option, includes the required key points, and professionally handles repair-only objections by validating the customer's choice.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Presents the upgrade but omits key value drivers (e.g., safety, EV readiness) or handles objections with slight resistance rather than validation.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to present the panel upgrade as the Best option, misses most value drivers, or pressures/argues with the customer over a repair-only decision.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative checks for and presents secondary opportunities (e.g., surge protection, GFCI updates, EV charger interest) on L2/L3 service calls where the primary system is newer.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the interaction on L2/L3 calls to verify the rep diagnoses the main issue first, then identifies and pitches secondary opportunities using the recommended script (e.g., 'While I was looking at this, I noticed...').

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Diagnoses the primary issue accurately, proactively identifies highly relevant secondary opportunities, and seamlessly presents them using the exact recommended low-pressure script.
evaluation_criteria.good: Checks for secondary opportunities (like surge protection or GFCI updates) on L2/L3 calls and offers them to the customer after diagnosing the main issue.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Identifies secondary opportunities but fails to present them effectively, or uses a high-pressure approach instead of the recommended add-on script.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to check for or present any secondary opportunities on L2/L3 calls, focusing strictly on the immediate issue without offering beneficial add-ons.

metrics.description: Measures whether the representative offers the Home Protection Plan (club membership) to every customer who is not already a member. The strict KPI target is a 100% offer rate on non-member calls.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the end of the interaction to verify if the representative pitched the Home Protection Plan to a non-member, ensuring they covered the cost ($19.99/month) and key benefits (annual electrical/roof inspections, 10% off, priority scheduling) using the recommended wrap-up script to hit the 100% offer rate target.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently seamlessly transitions into the membership offer before wrapping up, effectively communicating all key benefits (inspections, discounts, priority scheduling) and the exact monthly cost.
evaluation_criteria.good: Offers the club membership to non-members, clearly stating the cost and at least a majority of the benefits.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Mentions the club membership but forgets to state the price, omits key benefits, or fails to position it correctly as a simple add-on.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Completely fails to offer the Home Protection Plan to a customer who is not already a member.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative structures repair estimates using a specific Good, Better, Best tiered approach after visually reviewing findings with the customer.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the repair presentation to verify the rep shows photo evidence on a tablet and presents three scaled tiers: Good (spot repair of the immediate failure), Better (repair plus addressing nearby secondary wear), and Best (full section replacement to address the root cause and prevent recurrence).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Effectively uses tablet photos to transition into options, flawlessly building and explaining the Good (spot), Better (spot + secondary), and Best (full section) tiers to prevent recurrence.
evaluation_criteria.good: Uses photos to explain the issue and builds three options scaling from a spot repair (Good) to a comprehensive section replacement (Best).
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Presents multiple options but fails to properly scale them (e.g., omits addressing secondary wear in the middle tier) or does not use photos during the transition.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to provide tiered Good/Better/Best options for a repair call, offering only a single all-or-nothing fix.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative effectively transitions into a replacement presentation, presents material choices, explains their impact, and covers warranties, timelines, permits, and payment options.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the interaction during a replacement estimate to verify the rep references specific evidence to justify the replacement, presents material choices (e.g., shingle, tile, foam), explains impact on lifespan and value, and discusses warranty terms, the timeline/permit process, and available payment options.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Flawlessly transitions using clear evidence, thoroughly explains material choices and their long-term impact, and provides a comprehensive overview of warranties, timelines, permits, and payment options.
evaluation_criteria.good: References physical findings to justify replacement, presents material choices, and covers warranties, timeline/permits, and payment options.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Presents replacement options but misses key presentation components like warranty details, timeline/permit explanations, or payment options.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to present multiple choices, completely misses explaining warranties, timelines, and permits, or recommends replacement without referencing findings.

metrics.description: Evaluates the representative's ability to effectively and professionally navigate common customer objections, including price, spouse/partner consultation, competitor quotes, delays, and requests for the minimum option.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the interaction for appropriate objection handling: pivoting to minimum options for price concerns, offering callbacks/emails for spouse consultations, verifying scope for competitor quotes, explaining specific risks for delays, and accepting 'minimum' requests without pressure while documenting findings.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Flawlessly utilizes recommended responses for all objections, showing high empathy, ensuring apples-to-apples comparisons, explaining risks clearly, and booking minimum options without any high-pressure tactics.
evaluation_criteria.good: Effectively handles objections (price, spouse, competitor, delay, minimum) using the recommended guidelines and without pressuring the customer.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Addresses objections but misses key elements of the recommended response, such as failing to explain specific risks when a customer waits, or not verifying the scope of a competitor's quote.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Argues with the customer, uses high-pressure tactics when a minimum option is requested, or completely fails to address objections professionally.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative effectively asks a direct closing question, avoids volunteering delays, and remains silent to allow the customer to answer.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the closing interaction to verify the rep asks a direct question (e.g., 'Which option feels like the right fit for you today?'), does not volunteer phrases like 'take your time', and maintains silence immediately after asking.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Delivers a confident, direct closing question, completely avoids suggesting delays ('take your time'), and flawlessly utilizes silence to prompt a decision.
evaluation_criteria.good: Asks a direct closing question and remains silent, allowing the customer to respond without offering unnecessary delays.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Asks for the close but breaks the silence too early or nervously volunteers that the customer can 'take their time' before they object.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to ask a direct closing question entirely, or actively talks the customer out of a decision by immediately offering them time to think about it.

metrics.description: Evaluates whether the representative secures a customer signature on the work order in ServiceTitan and confirms project details prior to beginning any physical work.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the job ticket to verify a signature was captured before work started. Monitor interaction to ensure the rep confirms the scope of work, price, payment method, and start date (or follow-up timeline for estimates).

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Flawlessly secures signature before work begins and clearly confirms all key details (scope, price, payment, timeline) with the customer.
evaluation_criteria.good: Gets customer signature on the work order in ServiceTitan before starting work and confirms scope, price, and payment method.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Gets signature but fails to verbally confirm scope, price, or payment details, or does so after work has already minimally started.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to get a signature before starting work or fails to confirm scope and price with the customer.

metrics.description: Measures the representative's performance in wrapping up the service call, including walking the customer through the completed work with before/after photos, checking for additional needs, and requesting a review.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the post-job interaction to ensure the rep walks the customer through the work done, shows before/after photos, asks if there are any other electrical/roofing issues, and requests a Google review using the provided script.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently provides a thorough visual walkthrough using before/after photos, seamlessly checks for additional needs, and confidently requests a Google review using the exact script.
evaluation_criteria.good: Walks the customer through the completed work, shows photos, asks about other issues, and requests a review.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Completes a walkthrough but forgets to show photos, check for other issues, or skips the review request.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Leaves without walking the customer through the work done or completely fails to request a review when the job was successful.

metrics.description: Ensures the representative properly updates ServiceTitan with all final job details, required specific notes, and photos before leaving the property. The strict KPI target is 100% completion before departure.
metrics.evaluation_method: Audit the ServiceTitan job ticket post-completion to verify 100% compliance that the job status, detailed notes, three options/prices, sold outcome, required photos, and next steps are fully updated before the rep leaves the property.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Meticulously updates all fields in ServiceTitan (status, highly detailed notes including panel/roof specs, leak/faults, options, outcomes), comprehensive photos (at least 10, flagging inspected areas/faults), and clear next steps before leaving the property.
evaluation_criteria.good: Updates job status, required specific notes (panel/roof details, leak sources), options/outcomes, and required photos in ServiceTitan before leaving.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Leaves some required fields incomplete (e.g., missing specific meter/moisture readings, option prices, outcome reasons) or updates the system long after leaving the property.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Fails to update ServiceTitan with status, specific diagnostic notes (roof/panel specs), outcomes, or required photos, leaving the job ticket incomplete or inaccurate.

metrics.description: Measures the percentage of OP-tagged calls that result in a signed work order or sold job.
metrics.evaluation_method: Calculate the percentage of OP-tagged calls that successfully convert into a sale.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Achieves a conversion rate significantly above the 60% target on OP-tagged calls.
evaluation_criteria.good: Meets or exceeds the 60% conversion rate target for OP-tagged calls.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Conversion rate falls slightly below the 60% target.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Consistently fails to approach the 60% conversion rate target.

metrics.description: Measures the average revenue generated per electrical service job to ensure appropriate options and secondary opportunities are being presented.
metrics.evaluation_method: Calculate total revenue from electrical service jobs divided by the total number of electrical service jobs sold.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently achieves an average electrical job value well over $450.
evaluation_criteria.good: Meets or exceeds the $450 average job value target for electrical service calls.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Average electrical job value falls slightly below the $450 target.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Average electrical job value is significantly below the $450 target, indicating a failure to present options or add-ons.

metrics.description: Measures the average revenue generated from roofing inspections that lead to signed work.
metrics.evaluation_method: Calculate total revenue from signed roofing work divided by the number of converted roofing inspection jobs.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Consistently achieves an average roofing job value well over $2,500.
evaluation_criteria.good: Meets or exceeds the $2,500 average job value target for sold roofing work.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Average roofing job value falls slightly below the $2,500 target.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Average roofing job value is significantly below the $2,500 target, indicating missed opportunities in estimating or scope.

metrics.description: Measures the overall customer satisfaction based on post-job surveys.
metrics.evaluation_method: Review the average rating from post-job customer satisfaction surveys.

evaluation_criteria.excellent: Maintains an average post-job satisfaction score of 4.9 to 5.0.
evaluation_criteria.good: Meets or exceeds the target satisfaction score of 4.7 out of 5.0.
evaluation_criteria.needs_improvement: Average satisfaction score drops below 4.7 but remains above 4.0.
evaluation_criteria.poor: Average satisfaction score consistently falls below 4.0, indicating significant customer service issues.

qualification.scope_reason: The call consists of a voicemail left regarding an invoice, which indicates a post-contract billing or administrative matter rather than a new sales opportunity.
qualification.call_type_reasoning: The customer representative is leaving a voicemail following up about an invoice that was sent to the customer's email, indicating a post-sale or project follow-up interaction.

summary.summary: The customer representative reached the voicemail of David Lyons. The representative left a message informing the customer that an invoice had been sent to their email and requested a callback at 602-531-4185.
key_points: The call was routed to the voicemail of the homeowner, David Lyons.
The representative confirmed that an invoice was sent to the customer's email.
The representative requested a callback at 602-531-4185.

objections.objection_summary: Call classified as out-of-scope (non-sales). No objections extracted.

qualification.scope_reason: The transcript is a very short call entirely focused on routing/transferring to another staff member with no mention of services, estimates, or sales.
scope_signals: This is Angelica from the office
qualification.call_type_reasoning: The call is a brief administrative connection where a rep is returning a phone call. Given the recent interactions over the past two days were marked 'unqualified', this is an ongoing attempt to connect the prospect with a rep to qualify a new opportunity.

summary.summary: Angelica from the office contacted the customer, John, to inform him that a representative was returning his call. She confirmed she would facilitate the transfer and connect him directly with David, ending her portion of the call.
key_points: The customer, John, was expecting a return phone call from the company.
Angelica from the office called to transfer the customer directly to a representative named David.

objections.analysis_note: No potential objections found in transcript

qualification.follow_up_reason: Rep left a voicemail informing the customer that they are available to arrive early, within the next 30 to 45 minutes, for the scheduled roof inspection.
qualification.scope_reason: The call is a salesperson leaving a voicemail to coordinate the timing of a scheduled roof inspection/estimate appointment, which is an active part of the pre-sale pipeline.

letting you know i am available right now
qualification.call_type_reasoning: The representative is leaving a voicemail regarding an already scheduled appointment for today, updating the customer on their ETA and logistics.

Matt is available early and offered to arrive at the property within 30 to 45 minutes.
Matt provided a callback number of 602-722-09 for any questions.
next_steps: Customer to review the voicemail and contact Matt if there are any questions or issues with the early arrival.
Matt to arrive at the property for the scheduled roof assessment.
